page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.3xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 12 dedicated physical cores with 12 vCPUs and is managed via the KVM hypervisor. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 33 MB L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, BMI2, and FMA.

The server is configured with 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 6824.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 15004.59. While cached small-block memory read bandwidth is strong at 640.48 GB/sec, large-block uncached read operations drop to a weaker 36.23 GB/sec. Similarly, while small LLM inference performs adequately, medium-sized LLM inference is weak, processing prompts at 23.95 tokens/sec and generating text at 8.1 tokens/sec.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance represents a compute-focused resource allocation. It offers a balanced entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage. It is suitable for tasks such as software compilation, web serving, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded decompression workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.3xlarge is an x86_64 compute-focused server featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 24.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results position the instance in the middle tier for general compute, with a PassMark CPU score of 15004.59 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6824.0. However, it exhibits performance limitations in large-block uncached memory reads and medium LLM inference tasks. This server is best suited for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources with moderate memory requirements, such as web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.3xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 24.0 GB of RAM, and a 4 Gbps network baseline, optimized for compute-focused workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m2.3xlarge is a KVM-based server with 12 Intel Xeon cores, 24.0 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps bandwidth, designed for compute-focused tasks.
tagline: A dedicated twelve-core compute instance with a balanced memory profile for standard web serving and utility workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
24.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
No local storage or integrated GPU hardware
Suitable for web serving, software compilation, and database operations
